rulings.Case Title: JOCELYN WICKER VS. SAN FRANCISCO ANIMAL CARE AND CONTROL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER JOCELYN WICKER's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Petitioner Jocelyn Wicker's petition for writ of administrative mandate is off calendar without prejudice to being refiled. Ms. Wicker did not file a proof of service showing that she served her moving papers in compliance with CCP 1005. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. CAROLINE S LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Entering Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK's Motion For Order Entering Judgment Plaintiff American Express Centurion Bank's motion for order entering judgment is denied. Because the parties' stipulation recites that the debt allegedly owed by defendant Caroline Lee is disputed, plaintiff's effort to obtain a judgment for the full amount of the alleged debt rather than the unpaid amount per the parties' settlement runs afoul of the case law on unenforceable liquidated damages provisions in settlement agreements. (Viatech International, Inc. v. Sporn (2017) 16 Cal. App. 5th 796). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUDEN VS. C.R. BARD, INC., A NEW JERSEY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Cmpc And For Costs; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF WAYNE RUDEN'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Cmpc And For Costs (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Counsel are directed to meet and confer prior to the hearing and make a good faith effort to informally resolve the issue of the scope of subpoenas to plaintiff's three health insurers. Counsel are also directed to discuss plaintiff's proposal of a "First Look Agreement" to address the important question of whether insurance companies would carefully review fourteen years of private information to determine which records should be produced pursuant to properly circumscribed subpoenas. For guidance of the parties in the meet and confer conversation, the court makes the following observations: Under the allegations of the operative complaint, defendant is entitled to discovery related to plaintiff's claim for economic damages due to injuries caused by the Bard medical device at issue. Defendant California Pacific Medical Center is correct that certain amounts paid by plaintiff's health insurers may be admissible at trial under MICRA's exceptions to the collateral source rule. However plaintiff Wayne Ruden is entitled to object to overly broad discovery that violates his right to privacy. Although his allegations waive his right to privacy in some of his health insurance and medical records, the scope of the waiver must be narrowly construed and the scope of the inquiry permitted depends upon the nature of the injuries he has brought before the court. (Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 858-859, 863-864.) Here the nature of the injuries plaintiff has brought before the court are those he alleges were caused by an implanted Bard medical device. Thus the scope of the three subpoenas "must be narrowly circumscribed, drawn with narrow specificity, and must proceed by the least intrusive manner" to obtain discovery related to those specific injuries. (Davis v. Superior Court (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 1008, 1014.) (END OF PART 1 OF 2) ==(302/JPT)

(END OF PART 2 OF 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FUSIONSTORM VS. RENEE TENORIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees After Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT FUSIONSTORM's Motion For Attorneys Fees After Judgment Cross-defendant FusionStorm's motion for attorney's fees after judgment is denied. Although FusionStorm has neither waived nor is judicially estopped from seeking attorney's fees, it fails to identify a basis to obtain a fees award. A prevailing defendant on a FEHA claim is not entitled to an award of fees unless "unless the court finds the action was objectively without foundation when brought, or the plaintiff continued to litigate after it clearly became so." (Williams v. Chino Valley Independent Fire District (2015) 61 Cal. 4th 97, 115). The court's own review of the file in this case, including the papers filed on this motion, is insufficient to support a finding that Ms. Tenorio's FEHA claims were objectively without foundation when brought or she continued to litigate them after they clearly became so. Nor is FusionStorm entitled to fees per the fees provision in Ms. Tenorio's severance agreement because, by this lawsuit, FusionStorm did not recover any consideration paid to Ms. Tenorio per that agreement, which is the only basis for an award of contractual fees relied on by FusionStorm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Tenorio is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: FUSIONSTORM VS. RENEE TENORIO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FUSIONSTORM's Motion For Attorneys Fees Plaintiff FusionStorm's motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Although FusionStorm has neither waived nor is judicially estopped from seeking attorney's fees and it obtained injunctive relief on its Penal Code 502(c) claim, the court exercises its discretion per Penal Code 502(e)(1) not to award any fees to FusionStorm. FusionStorm never provided any persuasive evidence that Ms. Tenorio misused any of FusionStorm's information in a way that caused any harm. Moreover, the injunctive relief it sought and obtained was more prophylactic than remedial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Tenorio is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CALVIN MC PETERS VS. RICHARD PAGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFFs CALVIN MC PETERS and TRACY BOWERS' Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint Plaintiffs Calvin Mc Peters and Tracy Bowers' motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion in accordance with CRC 3.1324. Because plaintiffs did not file a proposed third amended complaint and it is not clear what changes they wish to make to their second amended complaint, the motion must be denied. As guidance for the parties going forward, but without prejudging a proposed pleading that the court has not yet seen it appears that plaintiffs may have good cause to allege a negligent retention claim against defendant Fisher Development Inc. and it appears that plaintiffs still lack sufficient facts to allege a viable request for punitive damages against Fisher. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MIKE BUCKLEY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Amended Responses To Plaintiff Mike Buckley'S Requests For Admission To Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Set One Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to Requests for Admission, Set One, Nos. 30-49 is granted in part. Defendants shall serve further amended responses omitting their objections that each request "calls for a legal conclusion." Requests for admission may call for the application of law to fact. ( CCP Sec. 2033.010.) The Court will rule on the remainder of the motion at the hearing. The parties to address (1) applicability of American Federation of State, County & Municipal Employees v. Metropolitan Water Dist. (2005) 126 Cal.App. 4th 247 and (2) conflicting evidence submitted in relation to plaintiffs' argument that defendants' amended responses were not verified. rulings.Case Title: TAMMY VANNORSDALL VS. ESPETUS CHURRASCARIA INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Paulo Ricardo Klein And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Klein And His Counsel In The Amount Of $6,260.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF TAMMY VANNORSDALL'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Paulo Ricardo Klein And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Klein And His Counsel In The Amount Of $6,260.00 Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to address the amount of sanctions to be awarded against defendant Paulo Ricardo Klein. Plaintiff's motion to compel Klein's deposition and the production of documents at deposition is granted. Defendant relies upon Liberty Mut. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 10 Cal.App. 4th 1282 to argue that he is an apex witness and therefore plaintiff must satisfy a heightened burden before being allowed to take his deposition. This argument is not persuasive. In Liberty, the defendant whose deposition was noticed sought a protective order under CCP Section 2025. (Id. at 1285.) The defendant was the head of a large national corporation and had no personal knowledge of the plaintiff's case or any of the facts alleged in the plaintiff's complaint. (Id. at 1285-1286.) Defendant Klein's situation is not the same. He did not move for a protective order. He has not shown that defendant Espetus Churrascaria, Inc., of which he is CEO and president, is a large national corporation. His claim to have no knowledge of facts related to plaintiff's allegations is contradicted by the corporate defendant's responses to interrogatories and requests for admission. Thus plaintiff is not required to meet a heightened burden before taking defendant Klein's deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELISEO DIMAS SOTO ET AL VS. FISH & FARM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT LIBRADA ZAVALA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant Librada Zavala's demurrer to the second and third causes of action alleged in the cross-complaint filed by Dar Nadia, Inc. is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The cross-complaint does not allege sufficient ultimate facts to support liability against Ms. Zavala for either conversion or violation of section 17200. If it can do so in good faith, Dar Nadia is given leave to correct this deficiency. rulings.Case Title: FINANCIALFORCE.COM, INC. VS. CLARITY COLUSTION GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 16, 2018, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT FINANCIALFORCE.COM, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Plaintiff and cross-defendant Financialforce.Com, Inc's demurrers to the cross-claim by defendant and cross-complainant Clarity Solution Group, LLC and to the second, fourth and fifth affirmative defenses in Clarity Solution Group, LLC's answer are overruled. The cross-claim has alleged with sufficient particularity who made some of the alleged misrepresentations regarding the features and capabilities of Financialforce.com's software product. (Cross-Complaint 5) The cross-claim also alleges when, how, and to whom these misrepresentations were made. (Cross-Complaint 4-,9). (See Leary v. Baker (1953) 119 Cal.App.2d 106, 109, "[i]It is settled that a single material misrepresentation knowingly made with intent to influence another into entering into a contract will, if believed and relied upon by the other, affords a complete ground for rescission. It is not necessary that there be a multitude of misrepresentations."]. The cross-claim has sufficiently pled intent to deceive and reliance on Financialforce.com's alleged omissions and misrepresentations. (C-C 7-18, 28) Based on the facts pled reasonable minds could differ as to whether reliance was justified, thus rendering the issue unsuitable for resolution on a demurrer. Fraudulent concealment has been sufficiently pled (C-C 9, 10, 16, 17, 18) where the duty to speak arose from the "defendant's exclusive knowledge of material facts not known to the plaintiff." Bigler-Engler v. Breg, Inc. (2017) 7 Cal.App.5th 276, 311. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Clarity is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If contested, the motion will be heard by Judge Fleming at 2pm in Department 302. =302/hk
